introduction: Inflammation of the gastric mucosa which may be acute or chronic. The most important risk factors for acute gastritis include drugs (NSAIDs) and alcohol. While H. pylori infection is the most important risk factor for chronic gastritis.

features: Upper abdominal burning pain or discomfort that mimics peptic ulcer disease (PUD).

investigations: Histology of gastric biopsy for definitive diagnosis

goals: Prevent progression to PUD or gastric cancer
drug: Magnesium trisilicate 1 - 2 tablets or suspension 10 mL orally three times daily or as required
Ranitidine 150 mg orally once daily as required
Omeprazole 20 mg orally once daily as required
supportive_measures: Endoscopic surveillance every 2 - 3 years for early detection of cancer

complications: Chronic gastritis: peptic ulcer disease; gastric cancer
